xposts.title: Life is the creation of meaning
xposts.excerpt: Life is the creation of meaning.

xposts.title: Life: self-organizing processes with intentional goals
xposts.excerpt: Life is a particular class of self organizing and self perpetuating processes that lead to complexity through intentional behavior (directed by informationally represented goals). This is the intended definition of meaning here.

xposts.title: Multiple senses of 'meaning'
xposts.excerpt: Depending on the context, meaning means eg. the assignment of a variable, the relationship between a pattern and changes in other patterns, the relationship of a thing to our model of everything (the universe), the relationship of an event to intentions or the task of an agent.

xposts.title: Chomsky disproven by statistical language models
xposts.excerpt: Chomsky's approach to linguistics has effectively been disproven by AI: 1. general learning is enough for language (no innate language skill necessary), 2. language is fundamentally scruffy (not neat), 3. language is enough to learn language (no grounding).

xposts.title: Language has scruffy heterogeneous meanings
xposts.excerpt: It's rare that a beautiful scientific paradigm fails as hard as Chomskyian linguistics, as evidenced in how statistical models superseded the work of computer linguists. Language has a neat skeleton but a scruffy body, grounded in a heterogeneous web of meanings.

xposts.title: Computationalism: semantics reducible to syntax
xposts.excerpt: At the core of computationalism lies the idea that all semantics must be fully reducible to syntactic operations.

xposts.title: AI develops formal concepts for mental states
xposts.excerpt: Artificial Intelligence is the only field that has developed formal concepts to define, analyze and naturalize mental states.

xposts.title: Ancient Greek layers of representation and understanding
xposts.excerpt: Ancient Greek thinkers developed theories of representation that distinguish between sensory images... abstract representation... reasoning. Aristotle's analysis of mental functionality did not just found psychology but artificial intelligence.

terms.title: meaning
terms.definition: Life is the creation of meaning via self-organizing processes with informationally represented goals and intentional behavior. Different contexts yield senses such as variable assignment, pattern relationships, or alignment with an agent's model of the universe and intentions.

terms.title: semantics
terms.definition: All semantics must be fully reducible to syntactic operations under computationalism; language models reveal semantics as grounded in a heterogeneous, scruffy web rather than neat innate structures.
